L1.subject: You watched the whole thing - here's a thank you
L1.methodology: Earned deal acknowledgment - recognizing real engagement deserves real reward
L1.content:
I noticed you watched the entire Do-With-You walkthrough.

That's not casual browsing. That's 12 minutes of actual attention.

So I want to offer you something I don't offer publicly:

**$399 instead of $499** - but only for the next 48 hours.

This isn't manufactured urgency. It's a genuine thank-you for taking the time to actually understand what I'm offering before deciding.

Here's the quick recap of what you'd get:
- Complete Local Bosses system setup
- 3 training sessions with me
- 30 days of support
- All the templates, all the automations

If you're ready: [Claim $100 off →]

If you're not ready, no worries. The standard offer will still be here.

But this specific deal expires {{deadline_timestamp}}.

- Jake

L1.subject: Quick question about what you watched
L1.methodology: Check-in that adds value - addressing likely hesitation points
L1.content:
Hey {{first_name}},

I wanted to check in on that $399 offer (expires in about 30 hours).

But more importantly - was there anything in the video that raised questions?

Most people who watch the whole thing but don't move forward usually fall into one of these:

**"I'm not sure I have time for 3 training sessions"**
We schedule around you. Most clients do early morning or evening. Sessions are 45 minutes each.

**"I tried something similar before and it didn't stick"**
That's usually because it was pure DIY. This is Do-With-You - I'm building it alongside you, not just handing you logins.

**"I need to think about the investment"**
Totally fair. $399 is real money. But if you're currently spending 5+ hours/week on manual follow-up, you'll get that back in month one.

Want to hop on a quick call to talk through whatever's on your mind?

[Schedule 15-min chat →]

Or if you're ready: [Lock in $399 →]

- Jake

L1.methodology: Gentle 12-hour warning on SMS for immediacy
L1.content: Hey {{first_name}} - your $399 DWY deal expires in about 12 hours. Just wanted to make sure you saw it before it's gone. No pressure either way: {{offer_link}}

L1.subject: Last call (then back to normal)
L1.methodology: Clean close - respects intelligence, leaves door open
L1.content:
{{first_name}},

This is the last note about the $399 deal - it expires in about an hour.

After that, Do-With-You goes back to the standard $499.

No hard feelings either way. You watched the whole video, you know exactly what this is. You're capable of deciding if it's right for you.

If it is: [Get started at $399 →]

If it's not the right time, the door's always open. Just reply whenever you're ready to talk.

Thanks for taking the time to really look at this.

- Jake

P.S. Even if you don't grab this deal, I'd genuinely love to know what held you back. Just hit reply - it helps me make this better for everyone.

L2.subject: You did your homework - here's what that unlocks
L2.methodology: Earned deal for serious buyer behavior - value stack reminder
L2.content:
{{first_name}},

You just spent real time understanding the Done-For-You system.

That kind of due diligence deserves a reward.

For the next 48 hours, I'm offering you:

**$1,199 instead of $1,499** - that's $300 off

PLUS a bonus that's not available anywhere else:
**Free Google Workspace setup** (normally a $150 add-on)

Quick reminder of what Done-For-You includes:

✓ Complete system built FOR you (not with you - for you)
✓ CRM, automations, follow-up sequences - all done
✓ Google Business optimization
✓ 60 days of support
✓ Training so you actually understand what you have

You don't lift a finger on setup. You just start using it.

This deal exists because you showed up as a serious buyer. Not a tire-kicker.

[Claim $300 off + free Google Workspace →]

Expires: {{deadline_timestamp}}

- Jake

L2.subject: What $1,199 actually gets you (broken down)
L2.methodology: Value justification - make the math obvious
L2.content:
{{first_name}},

I wanted to break down what $1,199 actually means for your business.

Here's what you're getting:

**The System (worth $2,000+ if you built it yourself)**
- Full CRM setup and configuration
- Automated follow-up sequences
- Review request system
- Lead capture forms
- Appointment scheduling
- Client communication hub

**The Optimization**
- Google Business Profile audit and fixes
- Local SEO foundations
- Online presence cleanup

**The Support**
- 60 days of direct access to me
- Training on everything we build
- Tweaks and adjustments as needed

**The Bonus (only with this deal)**
- Google Workspace setup ($150 value)
- Professional email, calendar, docs - all configured

If you hired someone to do all this separately: $3,000-5,000 easy.

If you tried to DIY it: 40-60 hours of your time.

$1,199 gets you done-and-working in about 2 weeks.

Still thinking it over? Reply and tell me what's on your mind.

[Lock in the deal →]

- Jake

L2.methodology: SMS reminder - 8 hours left, direct and respectful
L2.content: {{first_name}} - about 8 hours left on your DFY deal ($1,199 + free Google Workspace). After that it's back to $1,499 with no bonus. Here if you have questions: {{offer_link}}

L2.subject: Closing this out
L2.methodology: Clean close - no begging, door stays open
L2.content:
{{first_name}},

The Done-For-You deal expires in about an hour:
- $1,199 (instead of $1,499)
- Free Google Workspace setup

After that, it goes back to standard pricing.

I'm not going to pretend this is your "last chance ever" - the service will still exist. But this specific deal won't.

You did your research. You know if this fits.

If it does: [Get started →]

If it doesn't, genuinely no hard feelings. You can always reach out when the timing is better.

Appreciate you taking this seriously either way.

- Jake

L3.subject: Re: Your interest in fractional CTO services
L3.methodology: Enterprise tone - acknowledges serious evaluation, offers exclusive access
L3.content:
{{first_name}},

I noticed you spent considerable time reviewing the Fractional CTO offering.

That level of due diligence tells me you're evaluating this seriously - which is exactly the kind of client I work best with.

I'd like to offer you something I reserve for prospects who've done their homework:

**Extended Discovery Session**
Instead of the standard 30-minute call, I'm offering you a 60-minute deep-dive. No pitch, no pressure - just an honest assessment of whether fractional CTO support makes sense for your situation.

**Priority Scheduling**
I'm opening up calendar slots this week that aren't publicly available.

This offer is good for 72 hours - not because of artificial scarcity, but because I keep these slots limited to ensure I can actually deliver on them.

If you'd like to have a real conversation about your technical challenges and whether I can help:

[Schedule Extended Discovery →]

If you'd prefer to continue your evaluation, I understand. I'm happy to answer any questions via email as well.

Regards,
Jake Shore

L3.subject: A few things to consider before we talk
L3.methodology: Adds value during consideration period - positions as peer, not salesperson
L3.content:
{{first_name}},

I wanted to share some context that might help your evaluation - whether or not we end up working together.

**When Fractional CTO makes sense:**
- You're making technical decisions without technical expertise on your team
- You're spending money on development but not sure if you're getting value
- You need technical strategy but not a full-time hire
- Your tech is becoming a bottleneck but you're not sure why

**When it doesn't make sense:**
- You need someone writing code 40 hours/week
- Your challenges are purely operational, not technical
- You're not ready to act on recommendations

I turn away about 40% of prospects because the fit isn't right. That's not false modesty - it's just that fractional CTO works best in specific situations.

The extended discovery call I offered is genuinely about figuring out if this is one of those situations.

[Schedule the conversation →]

Or reply with questions - I'm happy to go back and forth via email if that's easier.

Regards,
Jake

L3.subject: Closing the loop on priority scheduling
L3.methodology: Professional close - no desperation, maintains positioning
L3.content:
{{first_name}},

The priority scheduling window I offered is closing - those calendar slots will go back into my regular availability.

The extended discovery call offer stands for a bit longer, but scheduling will be subject to normal availability (usually 1-2 weeks out).

If you'd like to continue the conversation: [Schedule here →]

If the timing isn't right, I completely understand. Complex technical decisions shouldn't be rushed.

Feel free to reach out whenever it makes sense on your end. I'm not going anywhere.

Regards,
Jake Shore

P.S. If you've decided this isn't the right fit, I'd appreciate knowing what factored into that decision. It helps me better communicate what I offer - and who I offer it to.

L4.subject: Looks like you're weighing options - let me help
L4.methodology: Helper positioning - noticed behavior, offering clarity not sales
L4.content:
{{first_name}},

I noticed you've been looking at a few different offers.

That's actually smart - the worst thing would be to pick something that doesn't fit your situation.

Let me help you think through this:

**Do-With-You ($499)** is right if:
- You want to understand how everything works
- You have 3-4 hours over the next month for training
- You want to be able to make changes yourself later
- You learn by doing

**Done-For-You ($1,499)** is right if:
- You just want it working, period
- Your time is worth more than the price difference
- You'd rather focus on running your business
- You'll learn by using, not by building

**Fractional CTO** is right if:
- You have ongoing technical decisions, not just a one-time setup
- You need strategic guidance, not just implementation
- Your challenges go beyond marketing systems

Here's my honest take: most local businesses do best with DWY or DFY. The CTO service is really for businesses with more complex technical needs.

Still not sure? Tell me about your business and I'll give you a straight answer.

[Reply to this email]

Or: [Book a quick call to talk it through →]

- Jake

L4.subject: Three questions to help you decide
L4.methodology: Self-sorting questions - helps them decide without us choosing for them
L4.content:
{{first_name}},

Still thinking about which option fits?

Here are three questions that usually make it clear:

**1. How do you feel about learning new software?**

A) "I actually enjoy it" → Do-With-You
B) "I'll do it if I have to" → Either works, but DFY saves headaches
C) "I'd rather not" → Done-For-You

**2. What's your time worth per hour?**

DWY requires about 4-5 hours of your time total.
DFY requires about 1 hour (just the intake call + training).

Difference: $1,000 for ~4 hours = $250/hour value.

If your time is worth more than $250/hour, DFY makes financial sense.
If it's worth less, DWY is the better deal.

**3. Do you want to be able to modify things yourself later?**

If yes → DWY teaches you the system
If no → DFY just gives you a working system

Most people know their answer after these three questions.

Still stuck? Just reply and tell me what you're wrestling with.

- Jake

L4.subject: No rush - just checking in
L4.methodology: Graceful close on comparison sequence - no pressure, open door
L4.content:
{{first_name}},

Just checking in on your decision process.

If you've figured out which option fits: great. Here are the links:
- [Do-With-You →]
- [Done-For-You →]
- [Fractional CTO Discovery Call →]

If you're still thinking: also great. These decisions shouldn't be rushed.

If you've decided none of these are right: totally fine. No hard feelings. I'd rather you find the right solution than buy something that doesn't fit.

The only thing I'd ask: if you're stuck on something specific, just tell me. Sometimes a 5-minute conversation clears up what feels like a big decision.

[Reply to this email] or [Book 15 minutes →]

Either way, I appreciate you taking the time to look at what I offer.

- Jake

metadata.purpose: Earned deal sequences triggered by genuine engagement behavior

trigger_conditions.L2: DFY video 100% watched OR 3+ offer page visits OR multiple CTA clicks
trigger_conditions.L3: CTO video 100% watched OR 3+ offer page visits OR multiple CTA clicks
